description: Shared parity fixture for is_substantive_sketch. Loaded by Python tests (tests/test_sketch_validation.py) and JS tests in Plan B for byte-for-byte parity. Each entry is {text, expected_substantive}.

entries.text: Plants take in light and somehow make sugar. Not sure where the water goes.
Photosynthesis happens in chloroplasts where light energy converts CO2 and water into glucose and oxygen.
I think it is when cells divide into two and each new cell has the same DNA.
A way of thinking about how you think — noticing your own learning patterns.
I think plants need light to grow but I'm not sure how that turns into food.
It happens in steps: light hits the leaf, water moves up, sugar comes out somehow.
Idk really, maybe something with light and water and the leaves do work?
